page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 22 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 11 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 88 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage for persistent disk volumes.

The x86_64 architecture of the underlying Xeon processor includes advanced instruction set extensions such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions, supporting bf16 and int8 data types. These hardware accelerators contribute to notable performance in specific workloads. In benchmark testing, the instance achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 12159 and a Passmark CPU score of 28524.38. Its memory subsystem recorded a read-only cached bandwidth of 1498.41 GB/sec and an uncached write bandwidth of 46.01 GB/sec.

The instance demonstrates solid capabilities in data processing and serving workloads. It achieved a static web serving throughput of 30.99 GB/sec and processed 24.14 million Redis SET operations per second. For machine learning tasks, the hardware-assisted matrix instructions enabled a text generation speed of 312.98 tokens per second on a small 135M parameter LLM, which represents a strong tier of performance relative to its resource class. However, for larger models like the 70B parameter LLM, prompt processing drops to 12.16 tokens per second, highlighting the limitations of running large-scale inference without dedicated GPU acceleration.

Given its balanced vCPU-to-memory ratio and lack of local storage, the c3-standard-22 is suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations. It offers a cost-effective profile for workloads that benefit from modern Intel instruction sets like AMX and AVX-512 without requiring the massive memory footprints of memory-optimized tiers or the high cost of dedicated GPU hardware.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 22 dedicated vCPUs and 88 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 11 physical cores with hyper-threading and supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. In benchmark evaluations, it delivers balanced performance, achieving a Passmark CPU score of 28524.38 and a static web serving throughput of 30.99 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong performance in small-scale LLM text generation, reaching 312.98 tokens per second on a 135M model. This instance is well-suited for web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications that require modern CPU instruction sets but do not demand local SSDs or GPU acceleration.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 22 vCPUs, 88 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It supports AMX and AVX-512 instructions for balanced enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22 features 22 vCPUs, 88 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, offering balanced performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced vector extensions for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 22 dedicated vCPUs and 88 GB RAM.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix math.
Delivers 30.99 GB/sec static web serving throughput and 24.14 million Redis SET operations per second.
Lacks local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware.
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and moderate database workloads.
